top_categories.workout_context: 13,123ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At mile 60 of 137, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Engadin Radmarathon's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
96ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 137 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Engadin Radmarathon. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 13,123ft of climbing on paved Swiss and Italian Alpine roads does to your power file on race day.
At Engadin Radmarathon, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Engadin Radmarathon's paved Swiss and Italian Alpine roads forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 137 miles.
137 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 137 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Tempo is Engadin Radmarathon's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 137 miles on paved Swiss and Italian Alpine roads. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Engadin Radmarathon’s 137 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Engadin Radmarathon with 13,123ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. High-altitude start at 1,473m — even the valleys are cool at 7:00 AM in early July; UV exposure is intense at altitude — sunscreen is critical above 2,000m.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Durability, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 137 miles of paved swiss and italian alpine roads in Zernez, Engadin Valley, Graubunden, Switzerland.
